pageContext.name: Confused Anime Butterfly Guy (Set of 2)
pageContext.description: Is this a pigeon?   Sideshow Art Prints presents the Confused Anime Butterfly Guy Canvas Art Print (Set of 2) by artist ukiyomemes, blending Japanese art history with modern meme culture to create a high-end and humorous addition to any art collection.  Ukiyo-e, or pictures of the floating world, is a Japanese art movement from the 17th century that emphasizes beauty and pleasure in life through a variety of subjects from history and folk tales. The style became popular with merchants prosperous enough to indulge in the privilege of decorating their homes with artwork — and now you can do the same, with a modern twist!   Artist ukiyomemes blends the classic art sensibilities with the most popular visual language of the current moment, internet memes. The Confused Anime Butterfly Guy Canvas Art Print (Set of 2) captures a ridiculous moment of misunderstanding in the natural world. The caption above the curious man reads, Kore wa hato desu ka, which translates to, Is this a pigeon? as he gestures to the beautiful creature before him. The image with the butterfly reads, Kore wa sumire desu ka, which translates to, Are these violets? further referencing the anime from which the meme originates.  The Confused Anime Butterfly Guy Canvas Art Print Set is officially licensed by Memes of the Floating World, AKA ukiyomemes, and each individual image comes printed on Aurora Natural Canvas, adding depth and texture to the composition. The gallery wrapped canvas prints also feature sawtooth hanging hardware installed for ease of display, and archival inks ensure the longevity of this hilarious scene in your collection.  Is this your next purchase? Elevate your humor to the floating world and bring home the Confused Anime Butterfly Guy Canvas Art Print (Set of 2) today!
